title: 10 AFT Notice of Change Transactions
body_text:
10	AFT Notice of Change Transactions
10.1	Overview of AFT Notice of Change Transactions
AFT Notice of Change (NOC) transactions are non-financial transactions used to notify AFT Originators (via the Originating Direct Clearers) of changes to the Transit Number or account number of an AFT Transaction. Once the AFT Originator updates this information, future AFT Transactions will be destined to the correct account.
Changes to an AFT Transactionâs Transit Number or account number may occur because of:
branch closures,
branch acquisitions,
account transfers within the same financial institutions, or
correction to the initial payment routing information.
Important
:	NOC transactions arenât created if the Payor/Payee changes from one financial institution to another.
See CPA Rule F1, Rules Applicable to Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) Transactions, and CPA Rule H1, Pre-Authorized Debits.
10.2	Creating AFT Notice of Change Transactions
Financial institutions create AFT NOC transactions on the Online Return System (ORS). See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures on creating NOC transactions on ORS.
For a list of NOC transactions created by the financial institution, see the NOC â Originated by Credit Union (NCOC/NUOC) report. See the AFT Reports Part for more information.
10.3	Receiving AFT Notice of Change Transactions
Financial institutions and AFT Originators need to review the Notice of Change (AFT) Detailed Listing (NCDL/NUDL) report for a list of NOC transactions sent by the Originating Direct Clearer. It is the responsibility of the Originating Direct Clearer to ensure the accuracy of the NOC transaction. Financial institutions and AFT Originators use the information from the NCDL/NUDL report to update their database for outgoing AFT Transactions on
PaymentStream
AFT or on alternative software. See the AFT Reports Part for information on the Notice of Change (AFT) Detailed Listing (NCDL/NUDL) report.
Important
:	CPA Rule F1, Rules Applicable to Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) Transactions, and Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits, state that action on incoming NOC transactions is mandated. Therefore, it is recommended that financial institutions and AFT Originators update their databases immediately when they receive an NOC transaction. This will ensure that AFT transactions are destined to the correct Transit Number or account number, avoiding delays in processing future AFT Transactions.
END
